blocks.content: Not the table — she steered Daisy toward the far side of the cafeteria, and Daisy let herself be steered.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: You're allowed to not be fine.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I know I'm allowed. I'm choosing fine.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: It was the water tower, Daisy.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: He didn't do anything wrong. We're not — there's nothing between us. He can go wherever he wants with whoever he wants.
She said it clearly and evenly. Savannah didn't argue.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: He doesn't have to do that.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: He kind of does. That's what Eli does.
Across the cafeteria, at the regular table, Kit was sitting with his lunch and not eating it.
